sections.label: Section 2.464
sections.original: ἐν δὲ μέσῳ κατέλαμπε σάκει φαέθων
sections.translation: In the center of the shield the sun ’ s bright circle

sections.label: Section 2.465
sections.original: κύκλος ἀελίοιο
sections.translation: was shining on winged horses , and the heavenly chorus of stars , Pleiades , Hyades , bringing defeat to the eyes of Hector ;

sections.label: Section 2.470
sections.original: ἐπὶ δὲ χρυσοτύπῳ κράνει
sections.translation: and upon his gold-forged helmet were sphinxes , bearing in their talons prey from singing . On his breast-plate a lioness , breathing flame , was eager in flight , with her claws ,

sections.label: Section 2.475
sections.original: ναῖον ὁρῶσα πῶλον .
sections.translation: seeing the colt of Pirene .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: ἄορι δʼ ἐν φονίῳ τετραβάμονες ἵπποι ἔπαλ-
sections.translation: On the bloody hilt four-footed horses were prancing , while over their backs black dust rose up . But your adultery killed the lord of such mighty warriors ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: ἔκανεν ἀνδρῶν , Τυνδαρίς ,
sections.translation: evil-minded daughter of Tyndareus ! For this the gods of heaven will one day send you to death ;

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: ἔτʼ ἔτι φόνιον ὑπὸ δέραν
sections.translation: truly the day will come when I shall see , under your red throat , blood gushing forth at the sword .

sections.label: Section 487
sections.original: ποῦ ποῦ νεᾶνις πότνιʼ ἐμὴ δέσποινά τε ,
sections.translation: Where , where is my young queen and mistress , Agamemnon ’ s child , whom I once brought up ? How steep is the approach to her house ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ῥυσῷ γέροντι τῷδε προσβῆναι ποδί .
sections.translation: for a wrinkled old man to ascend with this foot ! Still , for these friends , I must drag along my back bent double and sinking knees . Oh , daughter — for I see you now before the house — I have come , bringing you from my own sheep

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ποίμνης νεογνὸν θρέμμʼ ὑποσπάσας τόδε
sections.translation: this newborn nursling of the flock , having drawn it away from its mother , and garlands , and cheeses I have taken from the press , and this old treasure of Dionysus , furnished with fragrance , small , but sweet to pour a cup of it into a weaker drink .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: ἴτω φέρων τις τοῖς ξένοις τάδʼ ἐς δόμους ·
sections.translation: Let some one carry these gifts into the house for the guests . I have moistened my eyes with tears , and wish to wipe them off on this shred of my robe .

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: τί δʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , διάβροχον τόδʼ ὄμμʼ ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Why are your eyes wet , old man ? Have my troubles stirred your memory , after an interval of time ?

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ἢ τὰς Ὀρέστου τλήμονας φυγὰς στένεις
sections.translation: Or are you groaning over the sad exile of Orestes , and over my father , whom you once held in your arms and brought up , in vain for you and for your friends ?

sections.label: Section 508
sections.original: ἀνόνηθʼ · ὅμως δʼ οὖν τοῦτό γʼ οὐκ ἠνεσχόμην .
sections.translation: In vain ; but still I could not endure this : for I came to his grave , an addition to my journey ,

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: καὶ προσπεσὼν ἔκλαυσʼ ἐρημίας τυχών ,
sections.translation: and falling on it I wept for its desolation ; then I opened the wine-skin which I am bringing to the guests , and poured a libation , and set myrtle-sprigs round the tomb . On the alter itself I saw a black-fleeced ram as an offering , and there was blood , not long poured out ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ξανθῆς τε χαίτης βοστρύχους κεκαρμένους .
sections.translation: and severed locks of yellow hair . And I wondered , child , who ever dared come to the the tomb ; for it was no Argive at least . But perhaps your brother has somehow come secretly and on his return has done honor to his father ’ s wretched grave .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: σκέψαι δὲ χαίτην προστιθεῖσα σῇ κόμῃ ,
sections.translation: Go look to see if the color of the cut lock is the same as yours , putting it to your own hair ; it is usual for those who have the same paternal blood to have a close bodily resemblance in most points .

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: οὐκ ἄξιʼ ἀνδρός , ὦ γέρον , σοφοῦ λέγεις ,
sections.translation: Old man , your words are unworthy of a wise man ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: εἰ κρυπτὸν ἐς γῆν τήνδʼ ἂν Αἰγίσθου φόβῳ
sections.translation: if you think my own brave brother would come to this land secretly for fear of Aegisthus . Then , how will a lock of hair correspond , the one made to grow in the wrestling schools of a well-bred man , the other , a woman ’ s lock , by combing ? No , it is impossible .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: πολλοῖς δʼ ἂν εὕροις βοστρύχους ὁμοπτέρους
sections.translation: But you could find in many people hair very similar , although they are not of the same blood , old man .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἰς ἴχνος βᾶσʼ ἀρβύλης σκέψαι βάσιν
sections.translation: Then stand in the footprint and see if the tread of the boot will measure with your own foot , child .

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ἂν γένοιτʼ ἂν ἐν κραταιλέῳ πέδῳ
sections.translation: How could there be an imprint of feet on a stony plot of ground ?

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: γαίας ποδῶν ἔκμακτρον ; εἰ δʼ ἔστιν τόδε ,
sections.translation: And if there is , the foot of brother and sister would not be the same in size , for the male conquers .

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , εἰ καὶ γῆν κασίγνητος μολών ,
sections.translation: There is not , even if your brother , coming to this land . . . by which you might know your loom ’ s weaving ,

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἐν ᾧ ποτʼ αὐτὸν ἐξέκλεψα μὴ θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: in which I once stole him away from death ?

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθʼ , Ὀρέστης ἡνίκʼ ἐκπίπτει χθονός ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know that I was still young when Orestes was driven out of the land ? And even if I had woven him a robe , how could he , a child then , have the same one now , unless his clothes grew together with his body ?

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἤ τις αὐτοῦ τάφον ἐποικτίρας ξένος
sections.translation: But either a stranger , taking pity on his grave . . .

sections.label: Section 547
sections.original: οἱ δὲ ξένοι ποῦ ; βούλομαι γὰρ εἰσιδὼν
sections.translation: Where are the guests ? I want to see them and question them about your brother .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: οἵδʼ ἐκ δόμων βαίνουσι λαιψηρῷ ποδί .
sections.translation: There they are , coming quickly out of the house .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐγενεῖς μέν , ἐν δὲ κιβδήλῳ τόδε ·
sections.translation: They are well-born , but that may ring false ; for many of the well-born are base . However ; I give the guests welcome .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ γεραιέ . — τοῦ ποτʼ , Ἠλέκτρα , τόδε
sections.translation: Welcome , old man ! To which of your friends , Electra , does this ancient remnant of a man belong ?

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: οὗτος τὸν ἁμὸν πατέρʼ ἔθρεψεν , ὦ ξένε .
sections.translation: This is the one who brought up my father , stranger .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ὅδʼ ὃς σὸν ἐξέκλεψε σύγγονον ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? Is this the one who stole away your brother ?

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἔσθʼ ὁ σώσας κεῖνον , εἴπερ ἔστʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: This is the one who saved him , if indeed he is still alive .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Oh ! Why does he look at me , as if he were examining the clear mark impressed on a silver coin ? Is he comparing me to someone ?

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἴσως Ὀρέστου σʼ ἥλιχʼ ἥδεται βλέπων .
sections.translation: Perhaps he is glad to see in you a companion of Orestes .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: φίλου γε φωτός . τί δὲ κυκλεῖ πέριξ πόδα ;
sections.translation: A beloved man , yes . But why is he circling all around me ?

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: καὐτὴ τόδʼ εἰσορῶσα θαυμάζω , ξένε .
sections.translation: I too am amazed , looking at this , stranger .

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ , εὔχου , θύγατερ Ἠλέκτρα , θεοῖς .
sections.translation: O mistress , daughter Electra , pray to the gods .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: τί τῶν ἀπόντων ἢ τί τῶν ὄντων πέρι ;
sections.translation: For what thing , present or absent ?

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: λαβεῖν φίλον θησαυρόν , ὃν φαίνει θεός .
sections.translation: To have a beloved treasure , which the god is revealing .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: ἰδού · καλῶ θεούς . ἢ τί δὴ λέγεις , γέρον ;
sections.translation: See : I call on the gods . Or whatever do you mean , old man ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: βλέψον νυν ἐς τόνδʼ , ὦ τέκνον , τὸν φίλτατον .
sections.translation: Look now at this man , my child , your dearest one .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: πάλαι δέδορκα , μὴ σύ γʼ οὐκέτʼ εὖ φρονῇς .
sections.translation: I have been looking for a long time , to see whether you have lost your mind .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: οὐκ εὖ φρονῶ ʼγὼ σὸν κασίγνητον βλέπων ;
sections.translation: Lost my mind , because I see your brother ?

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας , ὦ γεραίʼ , ἀνέλπιστον λόγον ;
sections.translation: What do you mean , old man , by this word , unhoped for ?

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: ὁρᾶν Ὀρέστην τόνδε τὸν Ἀγαμέμνονος .
sections.translation: That I see Orestes here , Agamemnon ’ s son .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: ποῖον χαρακτῆρʼ εἰσιδών , ᾧ πείσομαι ;
sections.translation: What mark do you see , by which I shall be persuaded ?

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: οὐλὴν παρʼ ὀφρύν , ἥν ποτʼ ἐν πατρὸς δόμοις
sections.translation: A scar along his brow , where he fell and drew blood one day in his father ’ s home when chasing a fawn with you .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; ὁρῶ μὲν πτώματος τεκμήριον .
sections.translation: What are you saying ? I see the sign of the fall .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: ἔπειτα μέλλεις προσπίτνειν τοῖς φιλτάτοις ;
sections.translation: Then do you hesitate to embrace your dearest one ?

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκέτʼ , ὦ γεραιέ · συμβόλοισι γὰρ
sections.translation: Not any longer , old man ; for my heart is persuaded by your tokens . O you who have appeared at last , I hold you , beyond all hope .

sections.label: Section 579b
sections.original: κἀξ ἐμοῦ γʼ ἔχῃ χρόνῳ .
sections.translation: And you are held by me at last .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: οὐδέποτε δόξασα .
sections.translation: I never expected it .

sections.label: Section 580b
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐγὼ γὰρ ἤλπισα .
sections.translation: Nor did I hope .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: ἐκεῖνος εἶ σύ ;
sections.translation: Are you that one ?

sections.label: Section 581b
sections.original: σύμμαχός γέ σοι μόνος .
sections.translation: Yes , your one ally . If I draw back the cast of the net I am aiming for — but I have confidence ; or else we must no longer believe in gods , if wrong is to be victorious over right .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἔμολες ἔμολες , ὤ , χρόνιος ἁμέρα ,
sections.translation: You have come , you have come , oh , long-delayed day , you have lighted up , you have made visible a beacon to the city , who in long ago exile went forth from his father ’ s house , unhappily wandering .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: θεὸς αὖ θεὸς ἁμετέραν τις ἄγει
sections.translation: A god , now , a god brings our victory , my dear . Lift up your hands , lift up your words , send prayers to the gods for your brother with fortune , with fortune ,

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: κασίγνητον ἐμβατεῦσαι πόλιν .
sections.translation: to enter the city .
